Caitlin Hills.review_text: the most uncommunicative communications class there is. There's a lack of instructions for assignments & some are just incorrect. Links don't work, professors are late to meetings & they obviously grade using AI rather than reading work themselves. Also, the entire case competition is a research-driven project, not a communications-driven one.

Extremely unprofessional. Took her almost a week to respond to my emails. As a business communication professor lacks essential skills to even consider teaching the course. Provided unclear rubrics that she does not stick to. Extremely disappointed in a easy class about how much work it was just based on the professor.

Instructions on assignments are very unclear. It seemed the facilitator who graded my work was trying to find the smallest things to take points off for. I disagreed with most of what I got points off for. There were grades in the grade book that were not in the course schedule. Would not recommend.

Her videos say one thing but then the assignment says another. Very nit picky, she WILL find something wrong. This is the second class I have taken with her and I have never gotten a 100 in anything other than participation.

This class sucked. I had a project with 4 other people, across 4 different time zones and work schedules and we had to coordinate a live 15min presentation, given in the middle of the day on a Monday. I don't know if she's ever even considered we have jobs? There were also 2 checks in we had to do live on Zoom with her in the middle of the workday.
great and kind professor. Very fast feedback and had office hours every day.

No clear grading criteria or assignment instructions, and when asking for clarification you get no helpful answers and sometimes attitude. If you do the assignments correctly, you get a 90%, which makes no sense, shouldn't you get a 100%? There were multiple graders who graded differently and had different criteria so grading is very inconsistent.

Michael Mandel.review_text: While he is passionate and knowledgeable about presenting, he doesn't understand that BCOM is not everything. He needs to take a long, hard look at his teaching choices and realize that making students spend more time on BCOM than all other courses combined can be extremely detrimental to student success going forward, which is morally wrong.

Mandel grew on me throughout the semester. At first, he comes across as unnecessarily hard. In the end, it appears to be a "baptism by fire" thing. Mandel is more difficult than other BCOM professors which can be a good thing if approached right as he does want his students to succeed. BCOM will take up a ton of your time in 1st semester Eller.

EXTREMELY long time to grade, teaches what not to do rather than what to do, no clear grading/expectations (set up to fail), gives very rude/malicious feedback. unaccommodating throughout Covid. you'll never meet his expectations, ur never good enough. His grading was beyond unreasonable. hardest 1st sem eller class. UAccesss switch professors rn!!

This class was by far the most rewarding class I have ever taken. Mandel is a great professor that pushes you to grow and master your BCOM skills. This class can be overwhelming at times, but try to have a postive outlook because everything you learn in this class is a valuable skill. Mandel makes this class fun and is one of Eller's best!

He can be a little abrasive, especially in the feedback, but he isn't out to get you. He lightens up a bit on the grading as the semester progresses and it is possible to do well in this class as long as you put the work in. I'm personally not a fan of his teaching so I wouldn't take him again but he will set you up to succeed.

Was terrified of this class but gotta say I learned so much and walked away a better speaker. Work with him outside of class, use your resources, and put TIME into your group work, and an A is possible. Mandel and this class are tough, but just follow his guidance and trust the process. You will have to meet w/ your group almost everyday to succeed

Mandel is a touch professor who prides themselves in giving their students the skills to grow. This class was challenging at times but I learned so much along the way. Everything I learned I will take with me into the real world. 

The class has quick deadlines that can be overwhelming at times. Presentations and projects happen very fast.

Honestly not a good teacher. It's a business communication course (basically the only way he can grade you is if he likes how you write). MAJOR POINT: He purposely gives everyone bad grades in the beginning and then progressively increases your grade towards the end of the semester. Try and get a different teacher.

I love Mandel! He will push you to be better. He is a tough grader in the beginning but he wants to see you grow. You can get a lot out of this class if you work hard. The 1st half of the class is group work heavy, the 2nd half is individual. Tip: Use your preceptors! They know what he is looking for and can help you!

He is an extremely tough and nit-picky grader. Only a small portion of his students get A's in the class. It's almost as if it satisfies him to see his students fail, because he will fail you with any opportunity he gets. He also assigns an absurd amount of textbook readings every day. He is an unadaptable teacher. Run before it's too late.

Slow grader, unclear, inconcise, confusing expectations, taught the opposite of what was expected in case comp, not accomodating grade-wise, good bye mental health, domineering, zero extra credit, not caring or considerate, and said he cares. Readings and quizzes easy (very small part of grade). Teammates evals big part of grade!
Probably the worst teacher to take 314 with.

John Delaney.review_text: Delaney is a great guy and cares a lot about his students, but his grading expectations are tough. Yes, BCOM is a hard class, but Delaney expects you to go above and beyond for an A. If you follow his guidelines and rubric, it's a B. Expect to go to office hours often. Lectures are sometimes unclear but overall, Delaney is not the worst out there.
This is a hard class becuase it is meant to challenge you but John made the most of it! He was super kind and caring and his energy at 8AM is UNMATCHED!

If you get RANDOMLY placed on a bad group, GL. His takes off points on everything, very vague instructions, no helpful insights. The class is pure BS, no matter how hard they try to convience you website, personailty survays are benfital to your career. The truth is that NO ONE GIVES A SHT. This guy never worked a day in corporate, his is a joke.

One of the more time demanding classes during first semester of Eller. You have to really work to do well. The challenge was that he never made it clear what the expectations were for every assignment, resulting in receiving a grade of an 80% at best. It was difficult to assess your grade in the class because he takes months to grade anything.

Avoid having him at all costs! While it is possible to receive an A in the course, you have to work WAYYY too hard to do so. His grading criteria is extremely unclear and he takes an eternity to grade most assignments. Very passive aggressive personality too

Amber Richard.review_text: TAKE ANY OTHER PROFESSOR. The class lacked clear expectations and consistent evaluation. Grading often emphasized minor details over substance, and feedback did little to clarify how to improve. The overall experience felt frustrating and unnecessarily difficult. Unbelievable. No idea how someone rated her a 2. Way too high.

This was one of the most stressful classes I've taken. Expectations felt inconsistent, grading often didn't reflect the quality of the work, and presentations were docked for minor technical issues. Feedback was unclear, the overall tone felt dismissive, and support from course staff was lacking. Guidelines not allowing me to be more honest.
She's a great lecturer but a bit tyrannical. You'll lose points if you call her by her name instead of “professor”

Mikel Chertudi.review_text: He might be a super difficult grader but, it is because he cares he genuinely gives the best feedback that I have ever had. I truly feel like I became an amazing presenter.

Three things for you homies going into BCOM 314R. 1) This class is what you make of it. You can learn a lot if you work hard, and Mikel does an amazing job of inspiring his students to work hard. 2) Mikel wants to help you learn. If you go into his office hours on a weekly basis, you will find the class MUCH easier! 3) MIKEL IS THE BEST 314 TEACHER

At first, I had mixed feelings about Mikel when I started his class. I remember thinking it was going to be a long semester, and it definitely was, but looking back, he was the best person to help me grow as a presenter, especially for the case competition.

Mikel is an amazing professor. I would take his class again if I could! He cares about your success and gives you valuable feedback on your presentations. If you care to grow, he will help you get there. Make sure to go to office hours/set up meetings with him because he will help you and it'll get you closer to the A.

Mikel was incredibly helpful in this course. The boot camp writing assignments really prepared me to write more comfortably in a business environment. Mikel is always open to helping any student, so long as the student reaches out for help. The only negative about this class is he takes forever to grade and respond to emails.

One of the best BCOM Professor. Highly recommend to be in his class if you can. 
Use him the right way, be respected in his class, and you will obtain the right outcome

Mikel is an amazing teacher and one of the bests I have ever had. He is a hard grader, but he truly cares about the success of each of his students. He is extremely kind and tries to bond with his students. I have always been scared of public speaking, but by the end of his class, I have never felt more confident. Loved this class so much!

Mikel is an outstanding professor. He will always go the extra mile for his student's success. Although this is a difficult course, if you put in the time and effort, you will be successful. Take advantage of his workshops, feedback, team mentors, etc. Expectations are always clear! He is energetic and enthusiastic. You can tell Mikel truly cares.

If you have BCOM with Mikel you are in luck! He makes himself very available and will go out of his way to meet individually or with your team outside of class time. He genuinely cares about his student's success and gives great feedback and support to help you get there. BCOM is hard but Mikel makes it better, truly the best professor I've had yet

It doesn't get any better for BCOM than Mikel. He's tough but fair and you learn a lot more than you realize at first. If you are able to find the time, rehearse with your preceptor before your first presentation because trust me you are a worse presenter than you think and they will give you important feedback.

Mikel by far is the best professor I have had at the UofA. He really cares about you and wants you to succeed. This class could be tough and frustrating. However the work you put in will show what you get out of this class. Mikel is very accessible, make sure to go to office hours if you want an A in the course. By far one of the best professors!!!

I've genuinely never had a teacher who loves his job and cares about his students as much as Mikel. Mikel is definitely the hardest grader for BCOM, but if you put in the work you will succeed. This class is a time commitment but feels so rewarding after every presentation.
Extremely tough grader on the presentations.
mikel is the goat

Mikel was great at sharing deadlines, setting his expectations and grading criteria for the class, and giving in-depth presentation feedback. He is a great presenter himself, setting a great example for the rest of us. He helped all of us in the class bond better. Bonus points for the Crumbl cookies he brought us at the end of the semester.

I found him to be a fantastic professor. He gives very insightful and clear feedback, and is visibly dedicated to coaching his students to be the best they can be. His grading is tough, but there is a clear rubric backing it. If you read the assignment directions carefully and make sure to utilize his feedback, you'll do well in the class.

Ignore the bad reviews he was the best teacher I have ever had and this is my 3rd year at U of A. He goes out of his way outside of class to individualize his teaching for those who need it and made it so that any student that wanted to succeed could. Very friendly teacher, and very eager to help anyone who wants it!

Ignore the bad reviews this teacher is possibly the best teacher I've had so far in my 3 years at U of A. He truly cares about each students success and will go to great lengths outside of class to ensure that every student who wants to succeed can.

I had the pleasure of being with Mikel in the Spring of 2023. This class was very group-heavy. As in if one group member did not show up on presentation day your grade will get slammed. Also, there is a ton of work that Mikel pushes you to do great. I had good laughs with him he is a great guy but he expects excellence out of his students.

Mikel is one of the best professors I have had at the U of A. BCOM is a hard class and you definitely will need to work hard to finish with a good grade. Mikel gives good individual feedback and truly wants his students to succeed. I am so glad I got him as my professor for this class. BCOM has a lot of real world application as well.

BCOM is an intense class, but Mikel does a great job at motivating students to grow as professionals, which made the class that much more rewarding. If you truly put the work in, you will get an A. It's a grind, but totally doable. Mikel really cares about his students and does everything he can to help them succeed.
Challenging class but great professor. He truly cares about his students and you will do well if you are willing to work and have a growth mindset.
DO NOT TAKE THIS CLASS WITH THIS PROFESSOR!
Grades based on how much you kiss up and not the work you submit. pretty sure he just reads the name and assigns a grade based on that

Kimberly Marchesseault.review_text: Kim definitely made my first semester memorable and useful. She is tough on her students during the entire year but it really prepares you for case comp and general presentation skills. She is willing to meet with students during office hours and I suggest you get her feedback before turning in assignments.

Kim is okay. Way too loud and energetic in the morning. She grades pretty harshly, expecting you to really go above and beyond for even a 90, but once you understand what she wants it's not so bad. She's pretty personable and is definitely willing to help students on assignments in office hours. Seems to genuinely care about her students

A nice teacher and clearly enjoys what she does. She grades very strangely and will give you C's in the beginning of the semester because she "wants you to get better", although the content of my work never actually improved during the semester. Group presentations are 100% on your presenting skills and 0% on content. Felt like theater not business

Kim was a good professor but I believe she grades very harshly compared to other professors even in the BCOM department. Feedback is usually not the most helpful as it seems to be more general and less specific. This class requires a lot of work with your group so be prepared for that!

Nice lady, but she grades based off student dynamics and subjective emotions instead of her provided rubrics and examples. Her grading is so subjective that one of my slacker teammates (who skipped multiple meetings) ended up with an A and I ended up with a B! On some assignments, she took off points that weren't even on the rubric!

At first Kim is tough and intimidating, but it gets better I promise! You have to be willing to work hard if you want an A.  She has a little bit of a tiger mom mentality to push you and see what you are capable of, but in a good way. In the end, you learn a lot, and I found it to be the most useful class of my first semester in Eller.

Erin Paradis.review_text: Erin was a sweet teacher but I wasn't a fan of the class. She went too fast in lectures and exam questions were really confusing. Also follow EVERY bullet point on the rubric to get an A (preceptors don't always know what they're talking about). Other than that, I do think its an easy A

I absolutely loved Erin and she is easily my favorite professor I have had. She is very passionate about her class, making it easy to stay involved and get good feedback. That being said, BCOM314 is HARD and you will have to put in hours to get the grade you want. Take advantage of office hours and start projects long before they are due.
